verses.text: Baruch laments, 'Woe is me now! for the LORD hath added grief to my sorrow; I... find no rest.' His heart is overwhelmed. Faithful servants are not spared seasons of weariness and discouragement.
Baruch laments, 'Woe is me now! for the LORD hath added grief to my sorrow; I... find no rest.' His heart is overwhelmed. Faithful servants are not spared seasons of weariness and discouragement.
Baruch laments, 'Woe is me now! for the LORD hath added grief to my sorrow; I... find no rest.' His heart is overwhelmed. Faithful servants are not spared seasons of weariness and discouragement.

God answers that He is plucking up what He built, and asks, 'seekest thou great things for thyself? seek them not'; yet 'thy life will I give thee.' A gentle rebuke and a sure promise. In days of ruin, contentment and a preserved life are gift enough.
